name: Leadership & Vision
description: On leading people, casting vision, and rising to the moment.
author: Quips Editorial

quotes.content: A leader, he said, is like a shepherd. He stays behind the flock, letting the most nimble go out ahead, whereupon the others follow, not realizing that all along they are being directed from behind.
quotes.notes: Mandela recalls the axiom of the Thembu regent Jongintaba in his autobiography; the quoteDate reflects the book's publication year, not a precise day.

quotes.content: Plans are worthless, but planning is everything.
quotes.source: Remarks at the National Defense Executive Reserve Conference, Washington, D.C. (November 14, 1957)
quotes.notes: Eisenhower framed it as an old Army maxim he had heard, illustrating that the discipline of planning outlasts any single plan.

quotes.content: The price of greatness is responsibility.
quotes.notes: From Churchill's wartime speech receiving an honorary degree at Harvard, urging Anglo-American shared duty.

quotes.content: Leadership and learning are indispensable to each other.
quotes.source: Remarks prepared for delivery at the Trade Mart, Dallas (November 22, 1963)
quotes.notes: From the address Kennedy was to give the day he was assassinated; the text was released though never spoken.

quotes.content: A shared vision is not an idea. It is not even an important idea such as freedom. It is, rather, a force in people's hearts, a force of impressive power.
quotes.source: The Fifth Discipline: The Art and Practice of the Learning Organization (1990)
quotes.notes: Senge's definition of shared vision as a felt commitment rather than a slogan; quoteDate reflects publication year.

quotes.content: The first responsibility of a leader is to define reality. The last is to say thank you. In between, the leader is a servant.
quotes.notes: The opening frame of the former Herman Miller chairman's book on leadership as stewardship; quoteDate reflects publication year.

quotes.content: The servant-leader is servant first. It begins with the natural feeling that one wants to serve, to serve first.
quotes.notes: The founding statement of the servant-leadership movement, from Greenleaf's original essay; quoteDate reflects publication year.

quotes.content: A leader is best when people barely know he exists. When his work is done, his aim fulfilled, they will say: we did it ourselves.
quotes.source: Tao Te Ching, Chapter 17 (c. 4th century BC)
quotes.notes: A widely used English rendering of Chapter 17; exact wording (the attribution axis is sound) varies substantially across translations.

quotes.content: Management is doing things right; leadership is doing the right things.
quotes.notes: Commonly credited to Drucker and also to Warren Bennis; genuinely associated with both but not pinned to a single primary text.

quotes.content: Leadership is the art of getting someone else to do something you want done because he wants to do it.
quotes.source: Attributed to Eisenhower in speeches and interviews
quotes.notes: Long and consistently credited to Eisenhower, matching his views, but the exact original occasion is not firmly documented.

quotes.content: The day soldiers stop bringing you their problems is the day you have stopped leading them.
quotes.notes: Drawn from Powell's memoir and later leadership talks; wording varies slightly across retellings, so marked attributed.

quotes.content: Leadership is the capacity to translate vision into reality.
quotes.source: Attributed to Warren Bennis, leadership scholar
quotes.notes: Consistently credited to Bennis and true to his body of work, but not tied here to a specific book or page.

quotes.content: A leader takes people where they want to go. A great leader takes people where they don't necessarily want to go, but ought to be.
quotes.notes: Widely and specifically credited to the former First Lady; a precise original source is not established.

quotes.content: Before you are a leader, success is all about growing yourself. When you become a leader, success is all about growing others.
quotes.source: Attributed to Jack Welch, former GE CEO
quotes.notes: Frequently credited to Welch and consistent with his writing on developing people, but not pinned to a specific text.

quotes.content: Leadership is not about being in charge. It is about taking care of those in your charge.
quotes.source: Leaders Eat Last (2014) and related talks
quotes.notes: A signature Sinek theme expressed across his book and talks; exact phrasing varies, so marked attributed.

quotes.content: The best executive is the one who has sense enough to pick good men to do what he wants done, and self-restraint enough to keep from meddling with them while they do it.
quotes.notes: Long associated with Roosevelt and matching his views on delegation; a firm primary citation is not established here.

quotes.content: A leader is one who knows the way, goes the way, and shows the way.
quotes.source: Attributed to John C. Maxwell, leadership author
quotes.notes: A frequently repeated Maxwell line from his talks and writing; not tied here to a single book or page.

quotes.content: You manage things; you lead people.
quotes.source: Attributed to Rear Admiral Grace Hopper
quotes.notes: Consistently credited to Hopper, who often distinguished managing things from leading people; exact original wording varies.

quotes.content: The greatest leader is not necessarily the one who does the greatest things. He is the one that gets the people to do the greatest things.
quotes.notes: Widely credited to Reagan and echoing a broader leadership adage; a precise original occasion is not documented.

quotes.content: The key to successful leadership today is influence, not authority.
quotes.source: Attributed to Ken Blanchard, management author
quotes.notes: A recurring Blanchard theme across his books and talks; not fixed here to one primary source.

quotes.content: It is amazing what you can accomplish if you do not care who gets the credit.
quotes.notes: Popularly credited to Truman, who is said to have kept the sentiment on his desk, though the line predates him and is attributed to several people.

quotes.content: If you want to build a ship, don't drum up people to collect wood and don't assign them tasks and work, but rather teach them to long for the endless immensity of the sea.
quotes.source: Popularly attributed to Citadelle / The Wisdom of the Sands (1948)
quotes.notes: The wording axis fails: this is a popular paraphrase that does not match any exact passage in Saint-Exupery's text, though the sentiment derives from Citadelle.

quotes.content: Nearly all men can stand adversity, but if you want to test a man's character, give him power.
quotes.notes: The attribution axis fails: no primary Lincoln source exists for this line and it is widely regarded as a misattribution.

quotes.content: If your actions inspire others to dream more, learn more, do more and become more, you are a leader.
quotes.notes: The attribution axis fails: this line has no basis in Adams's writings and is a modern quotation without a documented primary source.

quotes.content: The function of leadership is to produce more leaders, not more followers.
quotes.notes: Consistently credited to Nader and in line with his public statements, but not tied here to a specific speech or text.

quotes.content: As we look ahead into the next century, leaders will be those who empower others.
quotes.notes: Widely credited to Gates and matching his stated views on organizations; a precise original source is not established.

quotes.content: A genuine leader is not a searcher for consensus but a molder of consensus.
quotes.source: Attributed to Martin Luther King Jr.
quotes.notes: Genuinely credited to King and consistent with his speeches, but the exact original occasion is not firmly pinned here.

quotes.content: Leaders aren't born, they are made. And they are made just like anything else, through hard work.
quotes.notes: Long associated with the Packers coach and true to his philosophy; not tied here to a single verified transcript.

quotes.content: Being powerful is like being a lady. If you have to tell people you are, you aren't.
quotes.notes: Famously credited to Thatcher, who repeated it; she presented it as a borrowed line, so the exact origin is uncertain.

quotes.content: Leadership is hard to define and good leadership even harder. But if you can get people to follow you to the ends of the earth, you are a great leader.
quotes.source: Attributed to Indra Nooyi, former PepsiCo CEO
quotes.notes: Credited to Nooyi from her interviews and talks on leadership; not fixed here to a single primary transcript.
